req.title: Diagnostic report (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-087 mandates three distinct verification axes: (1) a valid DiagnosticReport with populated Observation references is accepted and persisted (EP positive path); (2) a DiagnosticReport with an empty 'result' array is rejected with HTTP 422 (NEG / DT error path); (3) a DiagnosticReport with exactly one Observation reference sits at the minimum-valid boundary (BVA). A fourth test covers the AuditEvent emission end-to-end after a successful POST (STC: resource transitions from absent → created → audit-logged). Together these cases exercise Equivalence Partitioning, Boundary Value Analysis, Negative Path, Decision Table, and State Transition Coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4.

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_valid_observations_returns_201
preconditions: At least two Observation resources exist on the server
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing two valid Observation IDs
POST the payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and Location header
expected: Response body id field is non-empty
Location header points to the newly created resource
trace: R-HLT-087: clinician issues DiagnosticReport referencing Observations

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload with an empty 'result' array
POST the payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and OperationOutcome body
expected: Diagnostic message references missing Observation links
trace: R-HLT-087: missing Observation links shall be rejected
R-HLT-087: error pathway 422 when references empty
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture_clinician_auth_headers>

def test_diagnostic_report_with_empty_result_returns_422(clinician_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Lab Panel"},
        "result": []
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)
    assert any(
        "result" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower() or
        "observation" in i.get("diagnostics", "").lower()
        for i in issues
    )

tests.name: test_diagnostic_report_with_exactly_one_observation_is_accepted
preconditions: Exactly one Observation resource exists on the server
actions: Build a DiagnosticReport payload referencing exactly one Observation ID
POST the payload to /DiagnosticReport with clinician auth header
Capture HTTP response status and returned resource
expected: result array in response body contains exactly one entry
Entry reference matches the supplied Observation ID
trace: R-HLT-087: minimum valid cardinality for Observation references is 1
BVA lower boundary: 0 refs rejected, 1 ref accepted

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_after_diagnostic_report_creation
preconditions: At least one Observation resource exists on the server
actions: POST a valid DiagnosticReport referencing one Observation
Extract the created DiagnosticReport ID from the 201 response
Query /AuditEvent filtering by entity reference to the new DiagnosticReport ID
Assert at least one AuditEvent exists for the create operation
expected: At least one AuditEvent entry references the created DiagnosticReport ID
AuditEvent action is 'C' (create) or type system reflects create
trace: R-HLT-087: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
STC: state absent -> resource created -> audit log updated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture_clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def one_observation_id(clinician_headers):
    return <fixture_create_one_observation>(clinician_headers)

def test_audit_event_emitted_after_diagnostic_report_creation(
    clinician_headers, one_observation_id
):
    t0 = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"text": "Audit Test Panel"},
        "result": [{"reference": f"Observation/{one_observation_id}"}]
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE}/DiagnosticReport",
        json=payload, headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    dr_id = create_resp.json().get("id")
    assert dr_id
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"DiagnosticReport/{dr_id}"},
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    actions = [e["resource"].get("action") for e in entries if "resource" in e]
    assert "C" in actions
